reasoning_model_1: The audio content perfectly matches the script: "You're back? Why didn't you tell me!" (0.0s - 2.8s). The delivery is highly natural with excellent fluency. Regarding the core paralinguistic feature (Surprise), the model excels. At 0.0s-1.2s ("You're back?"), there is a sharp, breathy upward inflection that clearly conveys genuine shock. The follow-up question at 1.5s-2.8s maintains a high pitch and energetic tempo consistent with the urgency of a surprised reaction. The prosody feels very human-like.
reasoning_model_2: The audio content has a minor deviation; the model expands the contraction "You're" to "You are" (0.0s - 0.5s), resulting in "You are back?" instead of the requested text. In terms of fluency, the speech is slow and drags significantly, sounding somewhat mechanical and lethargic. Regarding the core feature (Surprise), the expression is weak. While there is a slight pitch rise on "back", the second sentence "Why didn't you tell me" (2.0s - 4.0s) is delivered with a flat, drawn-out tone that lacks the sudden energy or sharpness associated with surprise. It sounds more bored than surprised.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It accurately follows the text (including contractions) and delivers a very convincing, high-energy performance that captures the requested emotion of surprise through pitch variation and pacing. Model 2 fails to capture the energy of the emotion, sounding slow, mechanical, and bored. Additionally, Model 2 alters the text slightly ('You are' vs 'You're'). Model 1 demonstrates superior naturalness and emotional control.
